posture: non-commercial informational — MEDIUM risk for emblems, per master prompt line 59
hard_rules: Never co-brand a service mark with the site's own logo.
Every page rendering a service mark carries the non-endorsement disclaimer.
Never construct or guess an asset URL.

assets.format: PNG (unconfirmed — named in source research as army-logo.png / army-logo-horizontal.png)
assets.licensing_status: UNVERIFIED. Cannot confirm this file's existence or its exact license terms — the only cited source, war.gov/Resources/Branding-and-Trademarks/DOW-Trademark-Licensing-Guide/, returns HTTP 403 on every access attempt, including the war.gov root domain. General branch-logo doctrine (17 U.S.C. §105 no-copyright + 10 U.S.C. §8921-family trademark statutes + 32 CFR 765.14 safe-harbor disclaimer) would place a confirmed logo at MEDIUM risk, but that doctrine cannot be applied to a file whose existence is unverified.
assets.notes: Candidate filename reported by the research doc but never independently resolved. war.gov root and this sub-path both 403 on repeat testing (2 attempts, browser User-Agent, redirects followed). Do not use until independently confirmed reachable from a network without this block.

assets.notes: IMPORTANT: the research doc's own table places a third filename, 'space-force-logo.png', in its 'Seal image (restricted)' column — not the logo column. That file is therefore excluded entirely under the no-seal hard rule (description-based match on /seal/i), independent of its 403 status, and does not appear anywhere in this manifest. Only space-1.jpg / space-2.jpg (the doc's actual logo-column entries) are represented by this record. spaceforce.mil/About-Us/About-Space-Force/USSF-Symbols/ also 403.

Coast Guard emblem additionally noted by the research doc as 'available for commercial use only with a license agreement' (uscg.mil/Community/trademark/, also 403 on this test) — likely the most restrictive of the six even once/if a URL is confirmed.

assets.licensing_status: UNVERIFIED — no filename or path was ever supplied for this asset. The research doc describes it only as '(Eagle, Globe & Anchor — via USMC program)' with zero URL, so there was nothing to construct or test. Per ASSET POLICY this must never be invented.
assets.notes: Licensing contact per source research: trademark_licensing@usmc.mil / trademark.marines.mil (403 on this test) / hqmc.marines.mil Marine Corps Trademark Licensing Program page (403 on this test). USMC is described as the most aggressively-enforced of the six trademark offices (600+ registrations, up from 6 at founding).

assets.licensing_status: Public-domain federal work per 17 U.S.C. §105, per the source research — but this cannot be confirmed first-hand because none of the 3 candidate URLs were reachable in this test.
assets.notes: 3 candidates tested: war.gov/Resources/Insignia/ = 403; army.mil/e2/downloads/rv7/symbols/ranks.pdf = 403; dodig.mil Rank Chart_compliant.pdf = 403. Covers all six branches (Coast Guard insignia noted by source research as identical to Navy except color and the seaman-recruit single-stripe rank) — but no chart could be independently retrieved.

assets.format: HTML search portal — on-page raster images only; no bulk download; source research states 'all insignia are not yet available or in a high resolution/vector format'
assets.licensing_status: Public-domain federal work (17 U.S.C. §105) subject to insignia-protection statutes (18 U.S.C. Ch. 33; 32 CFR Part 507). TIOH's own site states graphic-image requests are provided 'as a courtesy to active and reserve military organizations only' — a civilian informational site does not clearly qualify for that courtesy channel, so treat requesting images directly from TIOH as unavailable to this project.
assets.notes: The ONLY asset-adjacent URL in the entire source research that independently verified reachable (HTTP 200, confirmed twice, real HTML search-portal content — not a WAF block page). However it is a searchable database, not an image API: no individual per-unit-insignia image URL exists anywhere in the source research to cite. Use strictly as a link-out ('view on the official TIOH catalog'); do not scrape, bulk-download, or re-host individual insignia images sourced from it. Covers all 6 branches plus ROTC/joint per source research.

assets.reachable: not tested — no candidate URL exists anywhere in the source research
assets.licensing_status: No official .mil endpoint publishes a downloadable specialty/warfare-badge image library (source research §4, explicit finding). TIOH catalog covers badges in principle (see tioh-unit-insignia-catalog) but under the same courtesy-access, no-bulk-download limits.
assets.notes: Recommended fallback per master prompt ASSET POLICY (line 310): prefer official DVIDS photography of a service member WEARING the badge (LOW risk) over an isolated badge graphic (MEDIUM-HIGH risk per source research). DVIDS pipeline is itself currently blocked for actual retrieval — see dvids_pipeline block.

assets.reachable: 200 (docs page only, https://api.dvidshub.net/docs); /search endpoint tested without api_key returns 403
assets.format: JPG — thumbnail via Search API (up to 2000px), full-resolution requires a second Asset API call per source research
assets.licensing_status: Public domain (17 U.S.C. §105); DVIDS API Terms of Service state assets are 'free for commercial use'; photographer credit requested.
assets.notes: Pipeline confirmed technically real — api.dvidshub.net/docs, dvidshub.net/about/copyright, and dvidshub.net/about/faq all returned HTTP 200 with genuine documentation content. But BLOCKED for actual asset retrieval: GET https://api.dvidshub.net/search?q=army with no api_key was independently tested and returned 403, confirming the key requirement. No individual image URL can be verified or cited without a registered, origin-locked key. See dvids_pipeline block for unblock steps.

disclaimer.site_wide: This website is an independent informational and career-guidance resource. It is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or authorized by the U.S. Department of War, the Department of Homeland Security, or any branch of the U.S. Armed Forces (Army, Marine Corps, Navy, Air Force, Space Force, or Coast Guard). All service names, seals, emblems, and insignia are the property of their respective services and are used here for informational and identification purposes only. Photographs are works of the U.S. Government in the public domain; credit to the originating service/photographer is provided where available.
disclaimer.per_page_short_form: [Service] emblem shown for informational purposes; not an endorsement.

dvids_pipeline.status: BLOCKED — no api_key; key is origin-locked to a production domain
known_constraints: search returns THUMBNAIL url only — full-res needs a 2nd Asset API hop
api_key is origin-locked; wrong origin returns 403
dvids_pipeline.licensing: public domain 17 USC 105; ToS: free for commercial use; photographer credit requested
dvids_pipeline.what_is_needed_to_unblock: A human must: (1) register a DVIDS member/developer account at dvidshub.net — this is an identity-verified web signup, not a public API endpoint, so it cannot be completed programmatically; (2) obtain a public api_key (format key-xxxxxxxxxxxxx) from the account's API-key management page; (3) register the site's production domain (protocol+host) as the key's authorized origin, since the key is origin-locked and every other origin gets 403 — independently confirmed here: GET https://api.dvidshub.net/search?q=army with no key returned 403; (4) store the key as a server-side secret, never in client-side/browser code, since origin-locking alone does not stop key theft from a public bundle; (5) budget a second Asset API call per selected image for full-resolution URLs, since Search only returns a thumbnail + webpage URL. Until steps 1-3 are done by the requester, no DVIDS image URL can be verified or added to this manifest.

fallback_in_effect: Original SVG emblems/badges authored for this project, rendered as clearly-labeled NON-OFFICIAL placeholders until official assets are unblocked.

completion_accountability: 46 named URLs were independently re-tested via curl (browser User-Agent, -L redirect-follow, 12s timeout) on 2026-07-12, plus 2 supplementary robustness checks (war.gov root retried twice; tioh.army.mil retried twice) and 1 confirmation check (api.dvidshub.net/search without a key). Result: 9 of 46 returned HTTP 200 (tioh.army.mil root + /Search.aspx; dvidshub.net/about/copyright + /about/faq; api.dvidshub.net/docs; law.cornell.edu x3; ecfr.gov x1). 37 of 46 returned HTTP 403, including the war.gov root domain itself (not just sub-paths) — independently reproducing, not merely restating, the pre-existing finding. Of the 10 AssetRecord entries in `assets`, 9 carry official_source_url: null because no candidate URL verified reachable; only tioh-unit-insignia-catalog carries a live URL, and even that is a search-portal link-out with no individual downloadable insignia image, not a ready-to-use asset. 7 seal-designated items (army-seal.png, marine-seal2.png, marine-seal3.png, navy-seal.png, the Air Force seal file at media.defense.gov, the Space Force file placed in the source doc's 'Seal image' column, sealCoastGuard.png) were filtered out under the hard no-seal rule and do not appear in `assets` at all, independent of their also-403 status. Net result: zero individually-verified, ready-to-download official visual assets exist as of this retrieval — DVIDS is the one genuinely live, documented, self-service pipeline (docs page and ToS pages all 200; public-domain/commercial-use licensing confirmed in writing) but is end-to-end BLOCKED for actual image retrieval pending a human-registered, origin-locked api_key (see dvids_pipeline.what_is_needed_to_unblock). The fallback_in_effect (original non-official SVG placeholders) is therefore the ONLY currently usable asset source for the build. This should be flagged to the requester: the master prompt STATUS TRACKER marks ASSETS '✔ COMPLETE,' but for practical build purposes the correct status is 'research complete, asset retrieval BLOCKED pending working URLs or a DVIDS api_key.'
